perturbation.original_text: to procure at the request of t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y f o r a n y o f those persons and bodies referred to in paragraph ሺbሻ ሺiiሻ and ሺivሻ to sign a separate confidentiality undertaking with the recipient in such form as the disclosing party may reasonably require prior to giving acc ess to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: to procure at the request of t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y f o r a n y o f those persons and bodies referred to in paragraph ሺbሻ ሺiiሻ to sign a separate confidentiality undertaking with the recipient in such form as the disclosing party may reasonably require prior to giving acc ess to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing reference to paragraph (b)(iv), the clause becomes contradictory because it only requires confidentiality undertakings for the recipient's auditors and professional advisors but not for third parties engaged by the recipient. This creates a contradiction regarding the level of confidentiality expected from different parties who have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure. ሺcሻ to ensure that all persons and bodies mentioned in paragraph ሺbሻ above a r e m a d e a w a r e , p r i o r t o t h e d i s c l o s u r e o f s u c h C o n f i d e n t i a l Information, of the confidential nature thereof, that they owe a duty of confidence to the disclosing party and agree to hold such Confi dential Information in confidence in accordance with the terms of this
perturbation.justification: The original text requires confidentiality undertakings for parties in paragraphs (b)(ii) and (b)(iv), including recipient's auditors, professional advisors, and third parties engaged by the recipient. The changed text omits paragraph (b)(iv), meaning third parties engaged by the recipient are no longer explicitly required to sign a confidentiality agreement. The contradicted text (section (c)) states that 'all persons and bodies mentioned in paragraph (b) above' are made aware of the confidential nature. By removing (b)(iv) from the modified text, it contradicts the requirement in contradicted text (section (c)) because not all parties with access to confidential information are required to agree to confidentiality, creating a contradiction in the expected levels of confidentiality, and fits the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clause 3.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and 5' from the end of the sentence, the clause creates a contradiction because it now allows retention of materials without explicitly requiring compliance with clause 5. Without compliance of Clause 5, this section now contradicts Section 5 itself, which states materials cannot be reproduced without consent, because now this clause allows them to keep it for archival purposes with no mentioning of the requirement of not reproducing them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All material containing Confidential Information furnished by o r obtained from the disclosing party, including without limitation, magnetic ta pes, documents, manuals, specifications, flowcharts, program listings and data file printouts, engineering drawings, architects plans, planning documents, shall be a n d r e m a i n t h e p r o p e r t y o f t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y a n d s h all not be reproduced in whole or part without the disclosing party's expr ess written
perturbation.justification: The original text requires compliance with Clauses 3 and 5 when retaining copies of confidential information. The changed text removes the requirement to comply with Clause 5. Clause 5 explicitly prohibits reproducing confidential information without consent. The changed text now allows retention of materials without requiring compliance with the reproduction prohibition in Clause 5, directly contradicting Clause 5's terms, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees not to circumv ent this agreement through the use of subterfuge involving the use of third parties. It is further un derstood that where any party contravenes the integr ity of this non‐circumvention p rovision, the contravening party shall: a.indemnify fully and pay over all commissions, fees or monies ob tained by the contravening party, directly or indirectly, to the party, directly or indirectly, to the party that was violated, and b.indemnify and to be liable for a ll legal fees for all parties, which may be incurred in the course of adjudication of said violation, and c.be subject to possible punitive a ction as awarded by the legal process. The signatories agree to keep confid ential the identity of any kind and all contacts so provided by any other signatories. Such identity including names, addresses, telephones, telefax n umbers, telex numbers or any ot her means, is the property of the introducing signatory and shall not be disclosed otherthan under the terms thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees not to circumv ent this agreement through the use of subterfuge involving the use of third parties. It is further un derstood that where any party contravenes the integr ity of this non‐circumvention p rovision, the contravening party shall: a.indemnify fully and pay over all commissions, fees or monies ob tained by the contravening party, directly or indirectly, to the party, directly or indirectly, to the party that was violated, and b.indemnify and to be liable for a ll legal fees for all parties, which may be incurred in the course of adjudication of said violation, and c.be subject to possible punitive a ction as awarded by the legal process.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section on Confidentiality of contacts, this causes a contradiction within the agreement. One portion states that contacts will not be disclosed, but the final portion of the clause creates a gap and removes the actual Confidentiality request of the contacts. This causes a in-text contradiction, within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to circumv ent this agreement through the use of subterfuge involving the use of third parties. It is further un derstood that where any party contravenes the integr ity of this non‐circumvention p rovision, the contravening party shall: a.indemnify fully and pay over all commissions, fees or monies ob tained by the contravening party, directly or indirectly, to the party, directly or indirectly, to the party that was violated, and b.indemnify and to be liable for a ll legal fees for all parties, which may be incurred in the course of adjudication of said violation, and c.be subject to possible punitive a ction as awarded by the legal process.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause regarding the confidentiality of contacts provided by signatories, which was removed in the changed text. The contradicted text, which is the original section of the contract, is then missing the confidentiality clause. This creates a direct conflict because one part of the document implies that contacts should be kept confidential (due to the original section), while another part (the changed text) no longer contains that requirement, which could be interpreted as allowing disclosure. This omission creates a logical inconsistency within the same section, as described in the explanation, by removing the confidentiality request of the contacts.
